chief_complaint: A 49-year-old man presented with severe dyspnea and left-sided fluidothorax.
history: The patient underwent pancreatoduodenectomy with pancreato-gastrostomy for chronic pancreatitis in 2018. Two years later, he developed a pancreaticopleural fistula, which failed conservative treatment. Total pancreatectomy with islet autotransplantation was performed to treat the fistula.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Pleuracentesis confirmed high amylase activity in pleural effusion. CT scan confirmed pancreaticopleural fistula. Islet autotransplantation was successful, with no need for insulin replacement therapy two years post-procedure.
